jokes.text: Were you just skyping with a horse? Not 'a horse,' Lemon. Rafalca, Mitt Romney's champion dressage mare.
jokes.setup: Liz questions what appears to be Jack talking to a horse
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact correction that it's specifically Romney's dressage horse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - treating absurd situation with complete seriousness. Topical political reference with absurdist twist.

jokes.text: She answers yes or no questions by eating either an apple for 'yes,' or a carrot for 'no.' If she eats both, that means 'life is full of unknowable gray areas.'
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how the horse communicates
jokes.punchline: The philosophical depth attributed to a horse eating both foods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple yes/no system to profound existential philosophy. Classic 30 Rock absurdism with perfect structure.

jokes.text: I saw the Today show. So I know how to make an autumn pizza that your teen will love.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she saw the news
jokes.punchline: Liz's focus on lifestyle content instead of actual news
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's disconnect from serious news. Sharp observation about morning TV priorities.

jokes.text: Paul Ryan dropped out of the race last night. What? Wow, why? Turns out he was actually born in Kenya.
jokes.setup: Paul Ryan dropping out seems serious
jokes.punchline: Absurd reversal of Obama birther conspiracy applied to Ryan
jokes.explanation: Clever political satire inverting the birther conspiracy. Good misdirection with efficient delivery.

jokes.text: Sunday Night Football is just Cleveland Browns games.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he's tanking NBC
jokes.punchline: Using universally disappointing Browns games as punishment viewing
jokes.explanation: Efficient sports humor playing on Browns' reputation. Perfect economy - maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: I'm allowing Jimmy Fallon to use his real voice.
jokes.setup: More ways Jack is sabotaging NBC
jokes.punchline: Implying Fallon's natural voice is somehow worse than his TV persona
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about NBC talent. Good character consistency for Jack's manipulative nature.

jokes.text: Yes, I have a reservation under 'black Hitler.'
jokes.setup: Liz saying the writers took 'no bad ideas' to heart
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate sketch premise delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Bold dark humor showing how bad TGS has gotten. Shocking but earned through setup about terrible ideas.

jokes.text: Criss and I have been taking the dump truck to the bone yard most nights.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about reproductive efforts
jokes.punchline: Liz's characteristically unsexy euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - making sex sound mechanical and unromantic. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Criss looks like a little elf prince.
jokes.setup: Jack telling Liz to enjoy it
jokes.punchline: Jack's oddly specific and somewhat condescending description
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jack's patronizing nature. The specific 'elf prince' description is memorably odd.

jokes.text: We'll just have to rely on observational humor. Like, um, 'table is a weird word.'
jokes.setup: Liz agreeing to avoid political material
jokes.punchline: Her pathetically weak example of observational comedy
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about bad comedy writing. Shows Liz's creative struggles through deliberately weak material.

jokes.text: And you gotta wonder who came up with the door. Bunch of cavemen sitting around. 'Hey, gronk! Before we starve to death in here, let's make a door!'
jokes.setup: Continuation of Liz's bad observational material
jokes.punchline: Absurd caveman logic about door invention
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad comedy showing Liz's desperation. The caveman bit feels forced, which is the point.

jokes.text: 'Cause you called me 'Mr. Clean's Gay Uncle' in front of my children.
jokes.setup: Pete claiming he's waited seven years to tell people what he thinks
jokes.punchline: Specific, emasculating insult Jenna previously used
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific insult that's both visual and emasculating. Good character work showing Pete's resentment.

jokes.text: This is my first royalty check for my summer dance jam, 'Balls.' ♪ Balls, balls, balls, balls, balls, balls, balls ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing her royalty check
jokes.punchline: The song is just the word 'balls' repeated
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna vanity with juvenile humor. The repetition works for character but feels cheap comedically.

jokes.text: you look like someone's been slowly poisoning Sally Field.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting Pete before reading her check
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and dark visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and dark insult. The 'slowly poisoning' detail makes it memorably vicious yet absurd.

jokes.text: Read it and weep, you mouth breathing, vag repellers.
jokes.setup: Jenna about to reveal her big paycheck
jokes.punchline: Crude but creative insult before the disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jenna's crudeness. 'Vag repellers' is memorable if juvenile.

jokes.text: It's $90, Jenna.
jokes.setup: Jenna's confident boasting about her big royalty check
jokes.punchline: The pathetically small amount deflates her completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure. The amount is small enough to be truly deflating after her buildup.

jokes.text: The FBI blamed it for a spike in summer sex crimes.
jokes.setup: Jenna defending how popular her song was
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dark consequence of her vapid song
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation. The idea that a silly song caused sex crimes is absurdist perfection.

jokes.text: Thanks to Al Gore's stupid Internet, people can just get music for free.
jokes.setup: Explaining why music industry isn't profitable
jokes.punchline: Blaming Al Gore for inventing the internet
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jenna's ignorance. Al Gore internet reference is dated but fits character.

jokes.text: ♪ Despite my beautiful singing voice ♪ ♪ and my amazing songwriting... ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining she got out of music
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself by singing about her talents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character work - can't resist showcasing herself even while claiming to be retired.

jokes.text: Fresh shave, elevated pulse, cologne. Mm-hmm. This man is here to do it on the desk.
jokes.setup: Tracy observing Criss's appearance and behavior
jokes.punchline: Deducing sexual intentions with detective-like precision
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character work showing his observational skills applied to inappropriate topics.

jokes.text: It's like I'm Don Draper and you're Megan. Okay, you're Don Draper. No, you're Glen and I'm Sally.
jokes.setup: Trying to make office sex romantic with Mad Men reference
jokes.punchline: Escalates to inappropriate child characters from the show
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows how Liz's attempts at romance go wrong. The child character reference is disturbing.

jokes.text: Then I would have seen Criss' muscular back. You think I care?
jokes.setup: Liz asking what if they'd been having sex
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's matter-of-fact response about not caring
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character beat showing his strange priorities and lack of normal social boundaries.

jokes.text: Well, okey-dokey-doo, America.
jokes.setup: Introduction of the vice presidential candidate
jokes.punchline: Completely unpresidential folksy expression
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Dunston's folksiness. Simple but effective for the political satire.

jokes.text: Did you guys see? I'm on TV but now I'm old.
jokes.setup: Tracy seeing his resemblance to Dunston
jokes.punchline: Focusing on aging rather than political implications
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character work showing his narcissistic focus on appearance over politics.

jokes.text: I sat on my testicles earlier and the heft of my body smashed them to smithereens.
jokes.setup: Tracy needs to take a moment
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of sitting injury
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy oversharing with dramatic language. 'Smithereens' elevates simple physical comedy.

jokes.text: I am having an actress side project crisis. So I'm not interested in whatever this is and whatever it thinks it's wearing.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to introduce his mother
jokes.punchline: Jenna dismissing both Kenneth's mother and her clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna selfishness and condescension. 'Whatever it thinks it's wearing' is particularly cruel.

jokes.text: You know the only celebrity I've ever met was Evel Knievel's motorcycle. And that was just a picture.
jokes.setup: Ron talking about meeting celebrities
jokes.punchline: The motorcycle isn't even a celebrity, and it was just a photo
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity - motorcycle to picture. Perfect character work for rural naivety.

jokes.text: Well, money's been tight ever since the market crashed into our house.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they haven't visited before
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of market crash
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay turning economic metaphor literal. Perfect economy and originality.

jokes.text: Do you know Evel Knievel's motorcycle picture?
jokes.setup: Ron trying to impress Jenna with celebrity connections
jokes.punchline: Referring back to his pathetic celebrity encounter
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ron's continued delusion about his celebrity connections.

jokes.text: See, that girl was in my car because I was trying to talk her out of being a prostitute
jokes.setup: Dunston on TV defending himself
jokes.punchline: Absurd and transparent excuse for suspicious situation
jokes.explanation: Standard political scandal humor. The excuse is transparently ridiculous as intended.

jokes.text: Hang on. I'm caught on a nail here. Okay, now we can continue.
jokes.setup: Dunston in middle of serious political statement
jokes.punchline: Interrupting for mundane physical problem
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Dunston's unprofessional nature. Timing is key to the humor.

jokes.text: NBC.com had over 100 hits today.
jokes.setup: Liz arguing everyone's talking about the resemblance
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small web traffic as evidence
jokes.explanation: Great joke about NBC's declining relevance. 100 hits is hilariously low for a major network.

jokes.text: Just like BP did when they heroically tried to lubricate the Gulf of Mexico.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how destroying something can save it
jokes.punchline: Reframing oil spill as heroic lubrication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark corporate doublespeak. Perfect Jack character work turning disaster into positive PR.

jokes.text: Are we better off now than we were four years ago? Not me. I now have herpes. And the Dixie mafia is trying to kill me.
jokes.setup: Classic campaign question about being better off
jokes.punchline: Personal disasters instead of political assessment
jokes.explanation: Good political satire subverting campaign rhetoric. The escalation from herpes to Dixie mafia is absurd.

jokes.text: Because you kept saying your Aunt Flo was in town. She was. Remember? I took her to MOMA and the cloisters?
jokes.setup: Misunderstanding about Liz's period tracking
jokes.punchline: Literal aunt visit confused with menstruation euphemism
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misunderstanding. The specific tourist activities make it funnier and more character-specific.

jokes.text: I assumed those were all vaginal euphemisms.
jokes.setup: Criss explaining his confusion
jokes.punchline: Treating museums as sexual euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Good followup to the misunderstanding. 'Vaginal euphemisms' is clinical and funny.

jokes.text: ♪ Your body is my garden of Eden ♪
jokes.setup: Criss trying to make sex romantic with a song
jokes.punchline: Cheesy religious/sexual metaphor sung earnestly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Criss's misguided romantic attempts. The religious metaphor is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: You just spoke in German. I don't think so, Criss.
jokes.setup: Liz apparently speaking German unknowingly
jokes.punchline: Liz's denial despite clear evidence
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist character beat showing Liz's stress. The unconscious German-speaking is surreal.

jokes.text: I'm caught on a nail here. Caught on a nail.
jokes.setup: Liz repeating Dunston's phrase
jokes.punchline: Shows she's unconsciously mimicking him
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Liz is absorbing Dunston's mannerisms from watching coverage.

jokes.text: Gift basket? Pears? Why?
jokes.setup: Liz receiving gift basket
jokes.punchline: Confusion about mundane fruit gift
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about random celebrity gifts. Works for character but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: You're supposed to say 'brava' to a woman. Oh, I am well aware of that.
jokes.setup: Liz correcting Jack's grammar
jokes.punchline: Jack deliberately used wrong form as insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Jack's passive-aggressive nature. The grammar correction backfires perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, did you hear that? That's the sound of honey boo boos piling up on your TiVo. No!
jokes.setup: Jack threatening Liz's free time
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to trashy TV accumulating
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference showing understanding of Liz's guilty pleasures. Topical but character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, but how could you? He's so amazing. He rides the train.
jokes.setup: Jack admitting political bias about Biden
jokes.punchline: Hilariously mundane reason for political admiration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire reducing complex politics to simple admiration. Perfect Jack elitism.

jokes.text: I remember the day he was born. He looked up at me and he said, 'mama, I am not a person. My body's just a flesh vessel for an immortal being whose name, if you heard it, would make you lose your mind.'
jokes.setup: Pearline sharing sweet memory of Kenneth's birth
jokes.punchline: Extremely disturbing supernatural revelation
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation from normal birth story to cosmic horror. Peak 30 Rock absurdism perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Let me die in an emergency room with a treatable disease like an American.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Obamacare extending life expectancy
jokes.punchline: Patriotic embrace of medical neglect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark political satire. Perfect economy turning healthcare debate into patriotic death wish.

jokes.text: we don't have a stereo anymore, on account of our mayor, who's a meth addict, took it apart in a field.
jokes.setup: Explaining their music situation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of meth-addicted mayor destroying property
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual reference to rural dysfunction. The specific detail of field disassembly makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It just so happens Ron and I have been to Key West. Oh, yes, we have. It was on our honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Defending Ron's tropical island songs
jokes.punchline: Reveals their romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that sets up Kenneth's shock. The honeymoon detail makes it more significant.

jokes.text: Look, Ken, I'm just trying to replace your dad... Oh, dang it. That's the wrong thing to say, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Ron trying to explain his relationship with Kenneth's mother
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizing he said exactly the wrong thing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Ron's social awkwardness. The self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, Paula never opens her eyes. So what I do is look at a nursing school catalog I keep under my pillow.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how to make sex fun
jokes.punchline: Frank's disturbing sexual fantasy involving medical students
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy showing Frank's creepiness. The nursing catalog detail is specific and disturbing.

jokes.text: Tracy will be red, Jenna is blue, and I will be green.
jokes.setup: Liz organizing complex schedule
jokes.punchline: Treating people like data points to be color-coded
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Liz's obsessive organizing. Sets up her sexual awakening through spreadsheets.

jokes.text: If I had a column for my basal body temperature, then I could schedule around peak ovulation. Right there. Yes, that is the spot.
jokes.setup: Liz getting excited about organization
jokes.punchline: Organizational talk becomes inadvertently sexual
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing Liz aroused by organization. Perfect double entendre with scheduling language.

jokes.text: If we print it in landscape, the page will take it all. Oh, yeah. It'll take it all. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Continuing the organizational arousal
jokes.punchline: Spreadsheet talk becomes explicitly sexual
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the organizational arousal. 'Take it all' is perfectly ambiguous.

jokes.text: I think I figured out what's been missing from my sex life. Organizing it.
jokes.setup: Liz's breakthrough about her sexual issues
jokes.punchline: Solution is typically Liz - more organization
jokes.explanation: Perfect character payoff. Liz's solution to intimacy issues is predictably bureaucratic.

jokes.text: Is that red wine with tonic water and olives in it? Yep. It's an old Spanish.
jokes.setup: Jack questioning Cooter's drink
jokes.punchline: Cooter's confident but clearly wrong cocktail knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Cooter's continued gullibility. The drink combination is appropriately gross.

jokes.text: just hanging out in hotel lobbies. If you wear a red vest, sometimes people think you're a valet and you can take a nap in their car.
jokes.setup: Cooter explaining his lobbying work
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation leads to car theft for naps
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing Cooter's confusion about lobbying. The valet scam is creative and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You know, just hanging out in hotel lobbies. If you wear a red vest, sometimes people think you're a valet and you can take a nap in their car.
jokes.setup: Cooter being a 'lobbyist'
jokes.punchline: The literal lobby interpretation and car napping scheme
jokes.explanation: Great Cooter character moment with perfect literal misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Cooter, this is a puffy frog with googly eyes. Oh, no. I've been handing those out all day.
jokes.setup: Cooter giving Jack a campaign sticker
jokes.punchline: Wrong stickers distributed to political contacts
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Cooter's incompetence. The frog stickers are absurd enough for the mix-up.

jokes.text: His long-time sexual relationship with the Hank Williams statue outside Montgomery city hall.
jokes.setup: Listing Dunston's problematic record
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual scandal involving statue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist political scandal. The specificity of the statue and location makes it funnier.

jokes.text: If you're only in New York for a few days, find a way to see Amar'e Stoudemire's penis. It's worth it.
jokes.setup: Being asked for New York advice
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate celebrity anatomy recommendation
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character work - inappropriate and celebrity-obsessed. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'll have my Jews on you so fast, you'll think you're an Asian girl.
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening legal action if Ron sues
jokes.punchline: Racially insensitive threat about lawyers and Asian stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor relying on racial stereotypes. Shows Jenna's awfulness but the joke itself is weak.

jokes.text: ♪ without a daddy, he's in danger ♪ ♪ he needs an older male stranger ♪
jokes.setup: Ron singing heartfelt song about Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Lyrics accidentally sound like predatory behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from innocent intentions sounding sinister. Good character work for Ron's naivety.

jokes.text: Ron, that was terrible. I'm going to be constructive here. You should kill yourself.
jokes.setup: Jenna offering constructive criticism
jokes.punchline: Suicide suggestion as music feedback
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna character work. The 'constructive' setup makes the brutal punchline funnier.

jokes.text: Comparing Ron to our inconsiderate neighbors, the Dingleberrys, is something I can do. But you are not allowed to.
jokes.setup: Pearline defending calling Ron a dingleberry
jokes.punchline: Literal neighbors named Dingleberry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay revelation. The neighbors actually being named Dingleberry is perfect absurdism.

jokes.text: Why, on the train right up here, I called you a bowling pin with a face drawn on it. A cheap albino lesbian. A finger with teeth. A hummel someone left on the radiator.
jokes.setup: Pearline explaining what family insults are like
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd insults for Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalating insults. Each one is perfectly specific and visual. Peak 30 Rock writing.

jokes.text: Do you remember that kid in school who bullied you? Well, I ate that goat.
jokes.setup: Pearline talking about defending Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Casual cannibalism as maternal protection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation from protective mother to cannibalism. Perfect economy and shock value.

jokes.text: I think I just got an idea for a song. It's called 'rum-soaked tampon.'
jokes.setup: Jenna inspired by the family conversation
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate song title
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's more shock than cleverness.

jokes.text: ♪ hey, girl ♪ ♪ me and you ♪ ♪ hey, baby ♪ ♪ this is just more proof ♪ ♪ that I am an amazing singer ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna performing new song
jokes.punchline: Song lyrics are just about how great she is at singing
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character work showing her narcissism. Meta-reference to her own talent is predictably self-absorbed.

jokes.text: ♪ does this song make you wanna do it? you're welcome ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna's new seduction song
jokes.punchline: The presumptuous and crude lyrics
jokes.explanation: Pure Jenna narcissism in song form, more cringe than clever.

jokes.text: ♪ I just want to lick your face, eeh... ♪
jokes.setup: Continuing the seduction song
jokes.punchline: The disturbing lyric and her own reaction to it
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with Jenna realizing she went too far.

jokes.text: Don't worry about the show. It's all scheduled. Look at this. Oh, yeah, mommy like.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her organization system
jokes.punchline: The sexual response to her scheduling
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of Liz's organizational fetish character development.

jokes.text: you can kiss goodbye your Dusseldorf bus schedule sex. You'll be having Paris Metro sex. Long sessions of afternoon love-making, followed by talking and making circles with your finger in Criss' chest hair.
jokes.setup: Warning Liz about losing her schedule
jokes.punchline: The transportation metaphors for sex styles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant transportation metaphor for sex that's both specific and absurd.

jokes.text: There are nine types of legitimate rape. One... a Halloween party.
jokes.setup: Dunston making campaign statement
jokes.punchline: Categorizing rape types starting with absurd example
jokes.explanation: Political satire referencing real rape comment controversies. Dark but topical for the political commentary.

jokes.text: Our opponent would like you to believe that Mitt Romney is a merman. Now, I know Mitt Romney does not live in the sea. A mormon, governor. A mormon? Now, that's crazy.
jokes.setup: Dunston defending Romney's religion
jokes.punchline: Mishearing Mormon as merman and finding merman more believable
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay political satire. The merman/Mormon confusion works well for Dunston's character.
